description: Define and implement the task scope for TASK-003 in the Agent Board VS Code extension. Because the task title and description are empty, the first responsibility of the implementing agent is to read .agent-board/project.json and .agent-board/tasks/TASK-003.json, infer the intended scope from surrounding tasks and recent activity, and write a concrete title and description back into the task JSON before any code changes. Once scope is captured, implement the change end-to-end across the extension backend (src/) and the React webview (webview/) as appropriate, preserving unknown fields in all .agent-board/ JSON files and updating relevantFiles, agentNotes, activityLog, and lastUpdated as work progresses. No real Claude or Codex API calls may be introduced.
acceptanceCriteria: TASK-003 JSON has a non-empty title and description that accurately reflect the implemented change, committed alongside the code.
Implementation lives in src/ for extension backend logic and webview/ for UI logic, following existing TypeScript and React patterns in the repo.
All reads and writes to .agent-board/*.json preserve unknown fields by round-tripping the parsed object rather than rewriting a fixed shape.
relevantFiles in TASK-003.json lists every file touched by the implementation using repo-relative paths.
activityLog in TASK-003.json contains at least one entry per status transition (in-progress, ready-for-qa) with ISO 8601 timestamps and the acting agent.
lastUpdated in TASK-003.json is refreshed on every write and matches the latest activityLog entry timestamp.
No code path invokes a real Claude or Codex network API; any agent interaction is file-based via .agent-board/.
npm run compile succeeds with zero TypeScript errors against the changed files.
qaChecklist: Open the Agent Board webview in an Extension Development Host and confirm TASK-003 renders with the new title and description.
Move TASK-003 through its status lifecycle in the UI and verify the task JSON file on disk reflects each transition with a fresh activityLog entry and updated lastUpdated.
Manually add an unknown field to TASK-003.json, trigger a write from the extension, and confirm the unknown field survives.
Reload the VS Code window and confirm TASK-003 state rehydrates from disk without loss.
Run npm run compile and confirm it exits 0.
Run npm run typecheck if defined and confirm it exits 0.
Grep the diff for fetch, axios, https.request, anthropic, and openai to confirm no real agent API calls were added.
designQaChecklist: TASK-003's card and detail view feel like an operational command centre: dense, scannable, no marketing chrome.
Primary task controls (claim, move to in-progress, move to ready-for-qa, complete) are visible without extra clicks or hover-only affordances.
Status, priority, assignedAgent, and qaAgent are legible at a glance in the board view.
Any new UI added for this task matches existing spacing, typography, and color tokens in webview/ rather than introducing one-off styles.
Empty states (no description, no relevantFiles) render gracefully instead of collapsing layout.
constraints: Do not call real Claude or Codex APIs; agent coordination stays file-based via .agent-board/.
Keep extension backend code in src/ and webview UI code in webview/; do not cross the boundary.
Preserve unknown fields in every .agent-board/*.json read-modify-write cycle.
Read .agent-board/project.json before claiming or implementing the task and reflect any relevant rules in the implementation.
Use the TASK-003 JSON file as the single source of truth for task state; do not duplicate state into another store.
Update relevantFiles, agentNotes, activityLog, and lastUpdated as the implementation progresses, not only at the end.
Run npm run compile and pass before transitioning TASK-003 to ready-for-qa.
Follow existing TypeScript and React patterns; do not introduce new frameworks, state libraries, or build tooling.
The task title and description must be populated in TASK-003.json before any code is written for the substantive change.
